titleStmt.title: SII 15.570: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 570. (B.K. No. 70 of 1927-28.) ḌAMBAḶ, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a pillar at the left of entrance into the Kalamēśvara temple.

It describes Māsavāḍi-nāḍu which is stated to have been situated on the northern bank of the river Dakshiṇa-Gaṅgā (i.e., Tuṅgabhadrā), in Kuntaḷa-vishaya. It then states that Permāḍi secured from his overlord the rulership of Māsavāḍi, by dint of his valour. Permāḍi had two sons Harihara and Kuppa and the latter was the father of Harihara and Mallidēva. The record is mutilated and incomplete.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© kuṁtaḷaviśayada dakṣiṇagaṁgānadiyōttara
tīraṁ bīḍidorpputirppu māsavāḍināḍadeṁ
tane . beḷasallavallavarisana meṇasu sarājā
navikṣuvrikṣamavellaṁ halasiḷe māmara satu
kularu sapuṇyarsvarūpigaḷu nararellaṁ .
Alli . priyadiṁ cakriya mecce vikramade pe
ttaṁ rūḍiyaṁ māsavāḍiya permmāḍi tadā
dyajaṁ hariharaṁ putraṁ tadīyānujaṁ na
yaśauryyaprabhu kuppabhūpatiḷaka kuppā
ḷāpamaṁ tatsutaṁ niyatāṁmnāyapa
dōcitaṁ hariharaṁ śrī mallidēvāgrajaṁ The record stop here.
Digital edition of SII 15.570 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
